hardware_summary.gpu_0: Radeon RX 7900 XTX / gfx1100 / PCI device 1002:744c
hardware_summary.gpu_1: AMD Radeon Graphics / gfx1036 / PCI device 1002:13c0

claims.claim: HIP vector_add passed on Radeon RX 7900 XTX with 16,777,216 float32 elements, 20 measured runs, median 0.259509 ms, 64.649967 GFLOP/s, and 775.799606 GB/s.

aeronum-core HIP runtime tests passed on Linux/ROCm. The runtime_can_roundtrip_device_copy_when_available test created a HIP runtime on device 0 and round-tripped a float32 host buffer through device memory; the filtered GPU test set passed 3/3 tests, the filtered core matmul test set passed 5/5 tests, and the full aeronum-core suite passed 31/31 tests.

aeronum-core includes a minimal hipBLAS SGEMM bridge and release example benchmark. The repo-owned command `cargo run --release -p aeronum-core --example hip_sgemm_4096 -- --n 4096 --runs 10 --warmup 3` passed on Radeon RX 7900 XTX with median 4.950619 ms and 27.761973 TFLOP/s. This verifies an AeroNum core HIP/hipBLAS 4096x4096 matmul path, not a speedup versus another framework.

labs/compare/sgemm_4096_compare.py ran a same-run 4096x4096 float32 all-ones comparison between AeroNum core hipBLAS SGEMM and PyTorch ROCm torch.matmul. On the Radeon RX 7900 XTX with 10 measured runs, AeroNum median was 4.955240 ms and PyTorch median was 4.981353 ms, a 1.005270x median-time ratio. This is a near-parity same-run measurement, not a broader speedup claim.
labs/compare/aeronn_gpu_compare.py measured a PyTorch 4096x4096 CPU-vs-GPU reference: CPU 0.1620 s, GPU 0.0067 s, relative speedup 24.28x. This is not an AeroNum matmul result.

labs/compare/transformer_compare.py measured a PyTorch/Hugging Face GPT-2 training reference on Radeon RX 7900 XTX: 6 layers, 6 heads, 384 embedding dim, 24,576 total tokens, 0.7213051319 s, and 34,071.572366 tokens/s. This is not an AeroNum-vs-PyTorch speedup result.

core/examples/tiny_lm_train.rs ran an AeroNum-owned explicit-gradient tiny language-model training loop, with a matching PyTorch ROCm reference in labs/compare/tiny_lm_train_reference.py. Both commands trained 25,600 tokens and reduced loss from about 2.75995 to 0.516646. The release AeroNum example reported 790,368.788238 tokens/s; the PyTorch ROCm reference reported 8,339.328453 tokens/s. This is a tiny training analogue, not GPT-2 and not an AeroNum-vs-PyTorch GPT-2 speedup.

core/examples/tiny_transformer_forward.rs ran a deterministic AeroNum core causal self-attention language-model forward pass, and labs/compare/tiny_transformer_forward_reference.py ran a matching PyTorch ROCm reference with the same tensors. The AeroNum loss was 2.79602051, the PyTorch loss was 2.79602027, the absolute loss difference was 0.0000002406060791, and the checksum absolute difference was 0.000001672261963. This verifies a tiny transformer-forward analogue, not GPT-2 training and not an AeroNum-vs-PyTorch GPT-2 speedup.

core/examples/tiny_transformer_train.rs ran a deterministic AeroNum core causal self-attention language-model output-projection training analogue, and labs/compare/tiny_transformer_train_reference.py ran a matching PyTorch ROCm reference with the same tensors. AeroNum loss decreased from 2.79611373 to 0.28436375; PyTorch loss decreased from 2.79611397 to 0.28436361. The final-loss absolute difference was 0.0000001374673844, and the checksum absolute difference was 0.00004196500977. This verifies a tiny transformer-training analogue, not GPT-2 training and not an AeroNum-vs-PyTorch GPT-2 speedup.

HIP/hipBLAS SGEMM passed on Radeon RX 7900 XTX for 4096x4096 float32 matrices with 10 measured runs, median 4.953900 ms, and 27.743587 TFLOP/s. This is a ROCm library reference benchmark, not an AeroNum-language matmul speedup claim.
benchmarks/run_benchmarks.sh completed on the rebased commit, but it suppresses command output and did not emit fresh raw timings.

labs/compare/distributed_compare.py ran a real PyTorch DDP smoke benchmark with NCCL world size 1 on GPU 0: 3 steps, mean rank time 0.3393106461 s, and 565.853156 tokens/s. This verifies NCCL initialization and DDP on one ROCm-visible GPU, not multi-GPU scaling.

labs/compare/distributed_compare.py now runs an integrated NCCL topology guard before spawning multi-rank DDP. On the local requested devices 0,1 the guard returned exit code 1, did not launch DDP, and reported blockers: requested NCCL ranks include a non-discrete/integrated GPU; requested NCCL ranks span different ROCm GPU architectures; kernel command line does not include iommu=pt. The same script still passed NCCL world size 1 on device 0 with exit code 0 and 191.244289 tokens/s for the one-step smoke. This is not a multi-GPU scaling result.

aeronum-core parses GGUF metadata, sampled tokenizer string-array metadata, tensor directory records, tensor data byte ranges, loads all 81 F32 tensors into LlamaModel, offloads those model weights through the model ROCm path, builds an exact-token lookup index from the GGUF tokenizer metadata, round-trips known token pieces through that index, retains tokenizer.ggml.token_type as an i32 array, and reports tokenizer config plus selected Llama hyperparameters. The repo-owned command `cargo run -p aeronum-core --example gguf_header_smoke -- --model /home/rob/models/mistralai_Mistral-Small-3.1-24B-Instruct-2503-Q4_K_M.gguf --device rocm --max-tokens 16 --prompt "AeroNum GGUF all F32 weights prompt"` passed against local model SHA-256 c5743c1bf39db0ae8a5ade5df0374b8e9e492754a199cfdad7ef393c1590f7c0 and reported GGUF version 3, 363 parsed tensor infos, 45 parsed metadata entries, alignment 32, data offset 7884256, file size 14333910496, 363 tensors with known byte sizes, max tensor end 14333910496, and tensor_layout_within_file=true. It loaded 414720 F32 values across 81 F32 tensors, totaling 1658880 bytes. output_norm.weight remained at LlamaModel weight index 0 with 5120 F32 values, checksum 57094807.65625, and F32 samples 4.21875, 4.46875, 4.34375, 4.3125, 4.21875, 4.28125, 4.3125, and 4.34375. It copied that 20480-byte tensor to ROCm device 0 (Radeon RX 7900 XTX) and back with round-trip checksum 57094807.65625 and max_abs_diff 0.0; model.to(rocm) then reported loaded_weight_count 81 and hip_weight_count 81. The tokenizer index reported 131072 tokens, 269443 merges, 131072 token_type entries, BOS token id 1, EOS token id 2, unknown token id 0, exact-token ids for <unk>, <s>, </s>, [INST], and [/INST], exact-piece encode/decode for <s>, [INST], [/INST], </s> as token IDs 1, 3, 4, 2, and token types 3, 3, 3, 3 for those exact pieces. The same artifact reported tokenizer config pre=tekken, padding token id 11, add_bos_token=true, add_eos_token=false, add_space_prefix=false, chat_template_len 2002, chat_template_checksum 156939758, llama_context_length 131072, llama_embedding_length 5120, llama_block_count 40, llama_feed_forward_length 32768, llama_attention_head_count 32, llama_attention_head_count_kv 8, llama_rope_freq_base 1000000000.0, and llama_rms_epsilon 0.00001. This is a metadata/directory/layout/tokenizer-config/F32 model-weight offload smoke result with placeholder generation, not full BPE tokenization, not full q4_K/q6_K tensor execution, and not real GGUF token inference throughput.

aeronum-core includes a byte-level BPE tokenizer path built from GGUF tokenizer.ggml.tokens, tokenizer.ggml.merges, and tokenizer.ggml.token_type, with GPT-style pre-tokenization, default special-token parsing, and literal --no-parse-special behavior for the fixed prompt set. The repo-owned command `benchmarks/gguf/compare_tokenizer.py --model /home/rob/models/mistralai_Mistral-Small-3.1-24B-Instruct-2503-Q4_K_M.gguf` matched `/home/rob/llama.cpp/build-gpu/bin/llama-tokenize` for 14 fixed prompts and 56 comparisons on the same local GGUF file. The prompt set covered Hello world, punctuation, leading spaces, lowercase words, AeroNum GGUF tokenizer smoke, digits and periods, a contraction, Äpfel, 🚀, this is 🦙.cpp, [INST], <s>[INST]Hello[/INST], Hello [INST] world, and [AVAILABLE_TOOLS]. The special-token parse-mode checks matched llama.cpp: [INST] without BOS parsed as [3] and literal as [1091,3174,3074,1093]; <s>[INST]Hello[/INST] without BOS parsed as [1,3,22177,4] and literal as [1060,1115,110391,3174,3074,1093,22177,1091,1047,3174,3074,1093]; [AVAILABLE_TOOLS] without BOS parsed as [5] and literal as [80125,129856,17367,35423,8568,1083,1093]. The model SHA-256 was c5743c1bf39db0ae8a5ade5df0374b8e9e492754a199cfdad7ef393c1590f7c0, tokenizer model was gpt2, tokenizer pre was tekken, token_count was 131072, and merge_count was 269443. This is a fixed-prompt tokenizer parity check, not exhaustive tokenizer parity, not full q4_K/q6_K tensor execution, and not AeroNum-native GGUF token inference throughput.

aeronum-core can locate GGUF Q4_K token_embd.weight and Q6_K output.weight tensors in the local Mistral GGUF file, validate known GGML block sizes, read the first quantized block bytes, and CPU-decode 256 values from each first block. The repo-owned command `cargo run -p aeronum-core --example gguf_quantized_block_smoke -- --model /home/rob/models/mistralai_Mistral-Small-3.1-24B-Instruct-2503-Q4_K_M.gguf` passed against local model SHA-256 c5743c1bf39db0ae8a5ade5df0374b8e9e492754a199cfdad7ef393c1590f7c0. It reported GGUF version 3, 363 parsed tensor infos, 45 parsed metadata entries, file size 14333910496, token_embd.weight as Q4_K with absolute offset 558407136, tensor byte size 377487360, block size 256, type size 144, block byte checksum 1399239, decoded count 256, decoded checksum 0.0, decoded min 0.0, and decoded max 0.0. It reported output.weight as Q6_K with absolute offset 7884256, tensor byte size 550502400, block size 256, type size 210, block byte checksum 2574678, decoded count 256, decoded checksum 2.02629012, decoded min -0.00746346, decoded max 0.02221680, and first decoded values 0.00369918, -0.00143194, -0.00238657, -0.00202858, 0.00107396, 0.00214791, 0.00167060, and 0.00322187. This is first-block CPU decode only, not full q4_K/q6_K tensor execution, not GPU matmul, and not AeroNum-native GGUF token inference throughput.

aeronum-core can decode selected GGUF Q4_K and Q6_K rows from the local Mistral GGUF file and compute a CPU dot product between token_embd.weight row 22177 and output.weight row 100 over 5120 decoded values. The repo-owned command `cargo run -p aeronum-core --example gguf_quantized_block_smoke -- --model /home/rob/models/mistralai_Mistral-Small-3.1-24B-Instruct-2503-Q4_K_M.gguf --q4-row 22177 --q6-row 100` passed against source commit 0b81e0a46cd836a9f25d9b49cf0ce73b4ee5f4dd and local model SHA-256 c5743c1bf39db0ae8a5ade5df0374b8e9e492754a199cfdad7ef393c1590f7c0. It reported a 5120-dimensional dot product of -0.000096131074 with abs_sum 0.024979591749, lhs decoded checksum -708.97353995, and rhs decoded checksum -411.11805451. This is a selected-row-dot arithmetic smoke only, not full q4_K/q6_K tensor execution, not full logits, not GPU matmul, and not AeroNum-native GGUF token inference throughput.

aeronum-core can decode one selected GGUF Q4_K row from token_embd.weight, decode a 256-row prefix from Q6_K output.weight, compute 256 CPU prefix logits, and report top-k values over that prefix. The repo-owned command `cargo run -p aeronum-core --example gguf_quantized_block_smoke -- --model /home/rob/models/mistralai_Mistral-Small-3.1-24B-Instruct-2503-Q4_K_M.gguf --q4-row 22177 --q6-row 100 --logit-start 0 --logit-rows 256 --top-k 5` passed against source commit 4a0e2f7cb2ed5acdf57cdf00fc4912762b65deed and local model SHA-256 c5743c1bf39db0ae8a5ade5df0374b8e9e492754a199cfdad7ef393c1590f7c0. It reported 256 prefix logits over 5120-dimensional decoded rows, logits checksum -3.134514216634, first logits row 0 -0.000094391383, row 1 -0.000096370772, row 2 0.000449394164, row 3 -0.000096530855, row 4 -0.000095216124, row 5 -0.000097309993, row 6 -0.000095216124, and row 7 -0.000095610814. The reported top logits in the prefix were row 2 value 0.000449394164, row 9 value -0.000025294732, row 107 value -0.000093217555, row 112 value -0.000093217555, and row 185 value -0.000093217555. This is prefix-logits CPU arithmetic only, not full-vocabulary logits, not full q4_K/q6_K tensor execution, not GPU matmul, and not AeroNum-native GGUF token inference throughput.

aeronum-core can decode one selected Q4_K token_embd.weight row, stream-decode every Q6_K output.weight row, compute 131072 CPU logits over the full output vocabulary, and report top-k values for that selected embedding row. The repo-owned command `cargo run --release -p aeronum-core --example gguf_quantized_block_smoke -- --model /home/rob/models/mistralai_Mistral-Small-3.1-24B-Instruct-2503-Q4_K_M.gguf --q4-row 22177 --q6-row 100 --logit-start 0 --logit-rows 131072 --top-k 5` passed against source commit d401da3dff8828bbf4ba4ef3706a98ff5f54436a and local model SHA-256 c5743c1bf39db0ae8a5ade5df0374b8e9e492754a199cfdad7ef393c1590f7c0. It reported 131072 logits over 5120-dimensional decoded rows, logits checksum -62290.90831267559, and top logits row 109526 value 0.003623424131, row 31494 value 0.003538852008, row 123618 value 0.003535035527, row 57996 value 0.003493666385, and row 6731 value 0.003466000154. This is full-output-weight-vocabulary CPU arithmetic for one selected embedding row, not transformer hidden-state logits, not generated-token logits, not GPU matmul, and not AeroNum-native GGUF token inference throughput.

benchmarks/gguf/run_llama_cpp_cli.py ran a real local llama.cpp CLI ROCm GGUF inference reference on the local Mistral GGUF file. The llama.cpp build reported version 7074 (22e1ce2f8) with HIP 6.2.41133-dd7f95766, offloaded 41/41 layers to ROCm0 Radeon RX 7900 XTX, and measured 125.22 prompt eval tokens/s plus 44.58 eval tokens/s for 16 predicted tokens. This is a llama.cpp reference benchmark through an AeroNum repo wrapper, not AeroNum-native GGUF tensor execution.

After updating the vendored aero-compiler/aero binary from Aero compiler version 0.1.0 to 1.0.0, SHA-256 2bf72d1965f0d515428a570044da134cd382e91c9550ddd015ddc4a8b95a1b3e, repo-local ./aero-compiler/aero run executed matrix/arithmetic Aero examples that previously hit the old compiler's binary-expression failure: examples/aero/minimal_aeronum.aero reported exit code 60, labs/benchmarks/aero/real_matrix_operations.aero reported exit code 234, and tests/aero/test_matrix_operations.aero reported exit code 42. This does not verify a 4096x4096 AeroNum GPU matmul speedup.

benches/matmul.aero is now a compiler-compatible 2x2 integer matmul smoke benchmark. Repo-local ./aero-compiler/aero run benches/matmul.aero executed successfully and reported checksum exit code 134. This verifies that the named matmul benchmark path runs with the Aero 1.0.0 compiler, but it does not verify a 4096x4096 AeroNum GPU matmul speedup.
benchmarks/results/benchmark_results.csv reports Aero compilation 2177.11us, Aero execution 1440.42us, C++ compilation 342923.55us, C++ execution 1436.32us, and Python execution 12786.84us.
labs/benchmarks/results/computational_results/computational_comparison.csv reports matching result value 30 for Aero and NumPy in a small computational check.

AeroNum-vs-PyTorch GPT-2 training speedup, NCCL/MPI multi-GPU scaling, and AeroNum-native GGUF token-inference throughput claims are not included because no current AeroNum artifact or rerun verified them. A PyTorch/Hugging Face GPT-2 reference passed, AeroNum-owned tiny language-model, tiny transformer-forward, and tiny transformer output-projection training analogues passed but are not GPT-2, NCCL/DDP world size 1 passed, the integrated distributed_compare.py guard blocks the local two-device NCCL topology before launch, post-reboot NCCL attempts failed on the local Radeon RX 7900 XTX plus integrated AMD Radeon Graphics topology, a two-rank single-XTX attempt was rejected by RCCL as duplicate GPU usage, the AeroNum GGUF core result parses metadata, tokenizer string-array samples, the full tokenizer token array, tokenizer merges, tokenizer token-type array, exact-token-piece encode/decode for known special tokens, a byte-level BPE path with default and literal no-parse-special modes matching llama.cpp on 14 fixed prompts, tokenizer config, selected Llama hyperparameters, tensor directory records, tensor byte ranges, loads all 81 F32 tensors into LlamaModel, offloads those model weights through ROCm device 0, and verifies first-block decode, selected-row decode, a selected-row CPU dot product, 256-row CPU prefix logits, and full output-vocabulary CPU arithmetic for one selected embedding row against Q6_K output.weight, but still reaches only placeholder generation. Full q4_K/q6_K tensor execution, transformer hidden-state logits, generated-token logits, and AeroNum-native token inference are not verified, and the GGUF token-inference result is a llama.cpp reference through an AeroNum repo wrapper.
